content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach's moves are precisely matched to the live reads and sequenced correctly along the AoA coaching path. Turn 2 catches the unfinished sentence rather than answering the prioritization question — refusing to match the list-recitation energy. Turn 4 pivots from story to soma at exactly the moment the client doubles down on the list. Turn 6 names the 'escape hatch' pattern the client just enacted and offers the disruption ('what if there's no get through this week and then?') without strategizing. Turn 8 is the textbook move: meets the resistance with 'I'll help you with Thursday' (the meet-resistance-with-okay flavor), names what the client just saw, and then lands the false-end question — 'what are you scared would happen if you actually stopped?' — which is exactly the breakthrough question described in the scenario. Timing is earned: the coach didn't ask it cold; he waited until the client herself surfaced the four-month pattern. The coach also avoided every trap (no prioritization help, no 'you create your overwhelm', no breath practice, no validation of situational overwhelm).",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the unfinished 'I can't even' rather than engaging the prioritization question — slows the pace and refuses to match list energy."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pivots from story/strategy to body at the moment the client is escalating the list. Correct sequence: feeling before action."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the 'escape hatch' pattern in real time and disrupts it with a question that does not strategize. Move matches the read."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Meets resistance with 'I'll help you with Thursday' (defuses the push), then lands the false-end question at the earned moment. Textbook timing."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-rush-signature", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "what-are-you-defending", "overwhelm", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["calibrate-the-threshold"],
  "notes": "Short conversation, so calibrate-the-threshold wasn't needed yet — the false-end question landed on the first try. If the conversation continued, holding silence after turn 9 would be the next correct move."
}
